---
title: Rafael N. Magalhães
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/profile_rounded.png
  image-width: 18rem
  image-shape: "rectangle"
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/rnmag
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/rmagalhães
    - icon: envelope-fill 
      text: Email
      href: mailto:rnunesmagalhaes@gmail.com
---

I am an Applied Scientist and statistical modeler specializing in probabilistic programming (Stan, R, Python) and Bayesian inference. I build bespoke mathematical models to extract reliable signals from noisy, observational data.
   
My approach to data is grounded in a rigorous academic foundation. I hold a PhD in Political Science from the University of São Paulo, with periods as a visiting researcher at Cambridge and Freie Universität Berlin. This training equipped me with deep expertise in research design, survey methodology, and **causal inference**—the exact tools required to measure true impact when standard A/B testing is impossible and data is inherently biased.

I combine this theoretical rigor with a decade of commercial experience. Since 2018, I have led an independent statistical consulting practice, successfully delivering over 30 end-to-end analytical projects. In this role, I don't just write math; I build systems. My work involves designing scalable data architectures, developing custom R/Python packages for internal teams, and translating complex statistical uncertainty into actionable strategy for C-level executives.

A core part of my philosophy is that good data science is collaborative. I treat code review like academic peer review, and I have been lucky to work with people motivated by genuine curiosity. Ultimately, my goal is to give the business a set of reliable "levers and dials"—tools that are statistically rigorous, computationally efficient, and practically useful for high-stakes forecasting.
